key_insight: An agentic, multi-step autoformalization system using general coding LLMs can translate research mathematics into machine-checked Lean 4 proofs, even when new concepts must be added first.
engineering_relevance: The framework suggests a practical path for scaling theorem-proving automation on real research papers by combining orchestration, library extension, and proof verification.
method: Orchestrator coordinates a multi-agent pipeline for research-level autoformalization.
Dynamically introduces missing type definitions and checks them with an Auxiliary Lemma technique.
Uses general-purpose coding LLMs rather than Lean-specialized models.
evidence_signal: The authors report machine-checked Lean proofs for 32 PutnamBench problems and successful formalization of main theorems from five STOC papers, with human validation and two proofs requiring no axioms beyond Lean’s kernel.
watch_item: Performance may depend on careful human validation and on correctly extending the formal library for out-of-scope concepts, which can add complexity and risk of hidden formalization errors.
paper_title: Beyond the Library: An Agentic Framework for Autoformalizing Research Mathematics
